rationale: Ground truth is thin (mostly non-technical thread plus one inline comment about verifying the reduce_scatter assertions in the layout test, which the diff already addresses). X explicitly calls out and validates exactly those reduce_scatter/device_communicator assertions at the relevant lines, and adds two concrete, file/line-anchored, actionable findings (missing test for the world_size<=1 guard branch; missing comment explaining the cfg_parallel_size>1 removal). Y produces a deeper technical correctness narrative (DPMetadata construction, cleanup safety) but never engages with the specific reduce_scatter test-coverage point reviewers actually raised, and its lone suggestion is a vague 'worth a comment' aside rather than a concrete change. Both are well-grounded with no fabrication, so precision is comparable, but X edges out on recall and actionability.
